meeting_purpose_blurb: Council makes official decisions in the form of ordinances, resolutions, or motions. Open to the public with public comment at designated times.
lead_headline: Council passes mental health co-responder funding, sets Candelis rezoning for July

type_specific.meeting_summary: The Arvada City Council held its May 19, 2026 business meeting, honoring several long-serving advisory board members and issuing proclamations for National Preservation Month and National Gun Violence Awareness Day. A lobbyist team presented a wrap-up of the 2026 Colorado legislative session, highlighting key bills affecting local government. The council approved all consent and resolution items unanimously, passed three ordinances on first reading, made two board appointments, and continued three rezoning hearings to July 7, 2026.
type_specific.lead_headline: Council passes mental health co-responder funding, sets Candelis rezoning for July
type_specific.meeting_purpose_blurb: Council makes official decisions in the form of ordinances, resolutions, or motions. Open to the public with public comment at designated times.

tonight_at_a_glance.synopsis: Council unanimously approved a cost-sharing software agreement and a $350,000 state grant renewal for its mental health co-responder program, advanced three ordinances to a June 16 public hearing, and continued three Candelis rezoning hearings to July 7.
highlights: Approved IGA with Arvada Fire District and City of Golden to share mental health co-responder case management software
Renewed $350,000 state grant funding for police co-responder (CREW) program
Three ordinances — water surcharge amendment, Barbara Nichols annexation, and Barbara Nichols rezoning — advanced to June 16 public hearing
Three Candelis parcel rezoning hearings (CB26-012, 013, 014) continued to July 7, 2026
Extensive public comment on Avalon Bay reconsideration request, data center regulations, climate action plan, and Frontline Farming lease termination

presentations.presenter_role: Representatives, Bodic and Castle Public Affairs (Arvada's legislative lobbying firm)
prose_paragraphs: Lobbyists Jennifer Castle and Ed Bowich briefed council on the just-concluded 2026 Colorado legislative session. Key themes included a $1.5 billion state budget deficit that limited new spending legislation, a Democratic trifecta at the Capitol, 626 bills introduced with roughly 500 passing, and significant legislative reaction to federal actions. Of particular relevance to Arvada, the HOME Act (affordable housing on school/transit/housing agency property without full local approval) passed and was signed by the governor, while two lot-size reduction bills were killed in the Senate — a win for local government control. Two data privacy bills that would have restricted law enforcement access to location and personal data were also defeated. The city's lobbying team took eight bill positions, six opposed, and reported a strong overall success rate. Looking ahead, presenters flagged a lengthy November 2026 ballot including a referred TABOR cap increase, a potential graduated income tax initiative, and a transportation funding initiative (Initiative 175), as well as significant turnover in the legislature due to the election year.

non_scheduled_public_comment.context_excerpt: I believe many residents are asking a simple question. If council had approved this application, would residents have been permitted to reopen the hearing with new arguments and hypothetical studies developed after the hearing simply because they disagreed with the outcome. Quasi judicial hearings are intended to evaluate a proposal based on evidentiary record presented during the hearing process.

consent_agenda.official_title: R26-037, a resolution authorizing an agreement between the city of Arvada and Brandon Construction Company for construction services related to the Pomona water main replacement project number 26-WA-1 in an amount not to exceed $2,744,469
consent_agenda.plain_english_summary: Approves a construction contract with Brandon Construction Company, up to $2,744,469, to replace aging water main pipes in the Pomona area.

consent_agenda.official_title: R26-038, a resolution authorizing an intergovernmental agreement between the city of Arvada and the city of Westminster for waterline improvements in connection with the city of Arvada's north trunk sewer improvements at S. Sheridan and 76th, project number 23-SR-3
consent_agenda.plain_english_summary: Approves a formal agreement with Westminster to coordinate waterline improvements tied to Arvada's north trunk sewer project near Sheridan Boulevard and 76th Avenue.

consent_agenda.official_title: R26-039, a resolution granting a non-exclusive public utility easement to the public service company of Colorado (Xcel Energy) to service utility lines and related fixtures and devices at 6901 Wadsworth Boulevard, Arvada 80003
consent_agenda.plain_english_summary: Grants Xcel Energy a permanent, non-exclusive easement to access and maintain utility lines at a city-owned property on Wadsworth Boulevard.

resolutions.official_title: R26-040, a resolution authorizing an intergovernmental agreement between the city of Arvada, Arvada Fire Protection District, and City of Golden for the use of a co-responder and mental health case management system provided by the company Gelata
resolutions.plain_english_summary: Allows Arvada to share its existing Gelata mental health case-management software contract with the Arvada Fire Protection District and City of Golden, reducing costs through cost-sharing and enabling information sharing across agencies that serve the same individuals in crisis.

resolutions.official_title: R26-041, a resolution authorizing contract amendment number seven to a state of Colorado behavioral health administration contract number 23IBH174364 to provide grant funding to the city of Arvada Police Department co-responder program
resolutions.plain_english_summary: Renews and increases state grant funding for the Arvada Police Department's civilian mental health co-responder (CREW) team to $350,000 for fiscal year 2027, covering staff salaries and related program costs.

resolutions.official_title: Appointment of Craig Kosan to the Metro Water Recovery Board of Directors for a two-year term beginning July 1, 2026 and ending June 30, 2028
resolutions.plain_english_summary: Reappoints Craig Kosan to represent Arvada on the Metro Water Recovery Board, a regional wastewater authority, for another two-year term.

resolutions.official_title: Appointment of Deborah Putsay to the housing advisory committee for an unexpired term ending April 30, 2027
resolutions.plain_english_summary: Appoints Deborah Putsay to fill an open seat on the city's Housing Advisory Committee through April 2027.

ordinances.official_title: CB26-018, an ordinance amending section 102-182, water surcharges, of chapter 102 utilities of the Arvada city code
ordinances.plain_english_summary: Would amend the city's water surcharge schedule; details to be presented at the June 16 public hearing.

ordinances.official_title: CB26-019, an ordinance annexing certain land into the city of Arvada, Barbara Nichols Plant Expansion Number Two, a 1.793 acre parcel of land located at 6290 West 56th Avenue in the county of Jefferson, state of Colorado
ordinances.plain_english_summary: Would annex a 1.793-acre parcel at 6290 West 56th Avenue from unincorporated Jefferson County into the city of Arvada; public hearing to follow.

ordinances.official_title: CB26-020, an ordinance rezoning certain land within the city of Arvada, Barbara Nichols Plant Expansion Number Two from Jefferson County R2 to City of Arvada IL (light industrial) and amending the official zoning maps of the city of Arvada, Colorado, a 1.793 acre parcel of land located at 6290 West 56th Avenue in the county of Jefferson, state of Colorado
ordinances.plain_english_summary: Would rezone the same 1.793-acre parcel at 6290 West 56th Avenue from Jefferson County R2 (residential) to Arvada IL (light industrial) upon annexation, reflecting its intended use for the Barbara Nichols plant expansion.

public_hearings.what_is_being_heard: Three ordinances — CB26-012, CB26-013, and CB26-014 — proposing to rezone multiple Candelis parcels (totaling roughly 62 acres) at the northeast and northwest corners of the Highway 72 and Highway 93 intersection from Planned Unit Development (PUD) to a mix of residential and commercial zoning designations (R24, R13, R6, and CG). No substantive testimony was taken; the hearings were opened solely to facilitate a vote to continue them to a new date.

vote_breakdown.motion_text: I move that council bill 26-012, 13, and 14 be continued July 7, 2026.

whip_count.name: Lauren Simpson
whip_count.rationale: Supported continuance; no stated individual rationale beyond procedural necessity.

public_hearings.follow_up_direction: All three hearings rescheduled to July 7, 2026.

meeting_summary: The Arvada City Council held its May 19, 2026 business meeting, honoring several long-serving advisory board members and issuing proclamations for National Preservation Month and National Gun Violence Awareness Day. A lobbyist team presented a wrap-up of the 2026 Colorado legislative session, highlighting key bills affecting local government. The council approved all consent and resolution items unanimously, passed three ordinances on first reading, made two board appointments, and continued three rezoning hearings to July 7, 2026.
